title: AutofilterXHam release signal config
description: Declarative configuration for scripts/check-release-signal.ps1. The script treats files as low-signal, high-signal, or conditionally classified based on token and semantic rule types.

$schema.description: Relative or absolute URI of this JSON Schema file. Example: ./release-signal-config.schema.json.

version.description: Config schema/data version consumed by the release signal script.

project.description: Project identifier used for human readability and guardrails.

highSignal.description: Rules that immediately classify matching files or paths as release-significant.

conditionalFiles.description: Rules for files whose release significance depends on the changed lines.

coverageAudit.description: Optional: Audits that critical shipped files/prefixes are covered by either highSignal or conditionalFiles.

requiredReleaseSignalFiles.description: Files that must be covered by highSignal or conditionalFiles.

requiredReleaseSignalPathPrefixes.description: Path prefixes that must be covered by highSignal or conditionalFiles.

reason.description: Human-readable explanation emitted in CI output when this rule is used.

lowSignal.description: Files and path prefixes that are considered low-signal unless labels override the result.

exactFiles.description: Exact repository-relative file paths treated as low-signal.

pathPrefixes.description: Repository-relative path prefixes treated as low-signal.

nonTrivialLines.description: Defines which changed lines are ignored before classification.

lineCommentPrefixes.description: Line prefixes ignored as comments after trimming.

blockCommentPrefixes.description: Block-comment or doc-comment prefixes ignored after trimming.

punctuationOnlyChars.description: If a trimmed changed line contains only these characters, it is ignored.

highSignalRule.description: A file, file list, or path-prefix rule that is always release-significant when changed.

userFacing.description: Whether the rule is expected to affect user-visible behavior.

file.description: Exact repository-relative file path this conditional rule applies to.

userFacing.description: Default user-facing value emitted by this conditional rule.

tokenAnyVsTokenAllRule.description: Classifies a file as release-significant if any user-facing token or JS action name is touched; classifies as helper-only if every non-trivial changed line contains a helper-only token.

userFacingTokens.description: Plain substrings. If any appears in the changed non-trivial text, the rule is release-significant.

helperOnlyTokens.description: Plain substrings. If all non-trivial changed lines contain one of these and no user-facing token matched, the rule is helper-only.

jsActionNames.description: Action names matched semantically as action: 'name' or action: "name".

cssVisibleVsCleanupRule.description: Classifies CSS changes as release-significant when selectors/properties imply visible behavior; classifies CSS-variable/token extraction as cleanup-only.

userFacingSelectors.description: Plain selector substrings that imply user-visible CSS behavior when touched.

userFacingProperties.description: CSS property families treated as visible behavior. A family also matches hyphenated sub-properties.

cleanupPropertiesUsingCssVariable.description: CSS property families treated as cleanup-only when the changed line uses var(--...).

metadataSelectorRule.description: Special rule for websites-metadata.js. Selector-array reformatting is non-release if selector keys and literal selector values are unchanged; metadata/parser/capability tokens are release-significant.

selectorKeys.description: Metadata object keys whose selector array formatting can be recognized semantically.

metadataFunctionPrefixes.description: Function-name prefixes treated as metadata/parser logic when matched as function <prefix>.

metadataTokens.description: Plain substrings indicating supported-site matching, capabilities, selectors, or parser logic.

labels.description: GitHub labels that force or suppress release significance.

docsPolicy.description: Documentation files used by the release signal docs policy checks.
